LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT GREG MCCORD, AN INDIVIDUAL AND DBA ON THE MOVE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION. DEFENDANT HAS PROVIDED ENOUGH EVIDENCE THAT CARROLL WAS AN INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF TO PLAINTIFF TO SHOW THAT CARROLL WAS NOT. PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED CONSIDERABLE EVIDENCE TO SHOW THAT CARROLL MAY NOT HAVE BEEN AN INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR BUT DEFENDANT'S EMPLOYEE. THEREFORE, TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT REMAIN ON THE BORELLO FACTORS. THE DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION AS WELL. DEFENDANT CLAIMS THAT PLAINTIFF HAS SUFFERED NO DAMAGES AND THEREFORE HAS NO STANDING TO BRING THIS CLAIM. PLAINTIFF, HOWEVER, HAS SHOWN THAT HE HAS SUFFERED AT LEAST $500 IN DAMAGE FOR THE DEDUCTIBLE HE HAD TO PAY TO HIS INSURANCE COMPANY AND PLAINTIFF'S PRAYER INCLUDED A PRAYER FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF AS WELL. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS OFF CALENDAR AS MOOT. THAT CLAIM WAS DISMISSED ON AUGUST 24, 2011. THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. IN ADDITION, THE COURT RULED ON PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE, ALL SUSTAINED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER ON MOTION.) JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-29-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT GREG MCCORD, an individual and dba ON THE MOVE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

APPEARANCES: Seth I. Rosenberg, Esq. of Minami Tamaki LLP 788-9000 for pltf & opposing party; M. Morgan West, Esq. of Toschi, Sidran, Collins Doyle 510/835-3400 for deft & moving party.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Defendant's motion for summary judgment/summary adjudication is denied as to the 1st cause of action. Defendant has provided enough evidence that Carroll was an independent CONTRACTOR to shift the burden of proof to plaintiff to show that Carroll was not. Plaintiff has presented considerable evidence to show that Carroll may not have been an independent contractor but defendant's employee. Therefore, triable issues of fact remain on the Borello factors. The defendant's motion for summary judgment/summary adjudication is denied as to the 2nd cause of action as well. Defendant claims that plaintiff has suffered no damages and therefore has no standing to bring this claim. Plaintiff, however, has shown that he has suffered at least $500 in damage for the deductible he had to pay to his insurance company and plaintiff's prayer included a prayer for injunctive relief as well. Defendant's motion for summary adjudication as to punitive damages is off calendar as moot. That claim was dismissed on August 24, 2011. The request for sanctions is denied. In addition, the Court ruled on plaintiff's objections to evidence, all sustained. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER on motion.) (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Off Calendar Dec-01-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Paul E. Van Dyk, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Kevin Carroll

No other appearance, tentative ruling adopted. Defendant Kevin Carroll's Motion to compel deposition of plaintiff and for sanctions granted:
1. Within fifteen days of notice of entry of this order, plaintiff shall appear and testify at his deposition, to be held at a date, time and place to be designated by defendant Carroll;
2. Request for sanctions granted; sanctions shall issue in the sum of $350.00 for attorneys' fees, and $327.99 for costs incurred, payable within thirty days of notice of entry of this order. Order signed in open court. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Paul E. Van Dyk, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Kevin David Carroll dba Tiburon Taxi

No other appearance, tentative ruling is adopted. Defendant Kevin Carroll's Motion to compel answers to interrogatories, production of documents and for sanctions granted:
1. All objections are stricken except attorney-client and attorney work product privilege;
2. Plaintiff shall serve full, complete and verified responses to defendant's interrogatories and request for production of documents within ten days of notice of entry of this order;
3. Request for sanctions granted; sanctions shall issue on the sum of $390.00; payable within thirty days of notice of entry of this order. Order signed in open court. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.
